title: Parasenecio tsinlingensis
taxonomy: Parasenecio tsinlingensis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification places it within the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants that are typically shrubs or annuals with small flowers. At the genus level, Parasenecio tsinlingensis is classified as a member of the genus Parasenecio, which is a group of plants that are closely related to the genus Senecio. The species is further classified as Parasenecio tsinlingensis, indicating its unique characteristics and taxonomic status. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics.

intro: Parasenecio tsinlingensis, also known as Tsinling Groundsel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Parasenecio tsinlingensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory distress.
